title: Ex Nihilo — System
summary:
Ex Nihilo (Cosmos) is the latest work in Casey REAS’ Still Life series, drawing on the five Platonic solids and Plato’s Theory of Forms. Plato posited a higher, invisible realm of perfect Forms, with the sensory world being an imitation. This work explores the dodecahedron, connecting Plato’s worldview to computer graphics where images are generated *ex nihilo* from code. Forms are instantiated through mathematical rules and procedural processes, appearing on screen as lines resolved into pixels, not depictions of objects.

In Still Life, Platonic solids are decomposed into pixels, their values translated into lines of varying position and color. From these simple rules emerge visual fields oscillating between structure and dissolution. Designed to run continuously, the work never repeats, operating as a kinetic system that unfolds over time.

Platonic Solids in Still Life: Cube → EARTH, Tetrahedron → FIRE, Octahedron → AIR, Dodecahedron → COSMOS, Icosahedron → WATER.

This work is co-presented by Art Blocks and Feral File in March 2026. The Still Life exhibitions began in 2016 at bitforms, New York.

Key Commands: 'R' — Show/hide Red channel, 'G' — Show/hide Green channel, 'B' — Show/hide Blue channel, 'W' — Toggle colors to White, 'S' — Adjust Speed, 'P' — Pause, Spacebar — Generate a new Still Life
